# EXECUTION-SPEC Frontmatter
# Generated by build-pipeline for PRD-052

prd: PRD-052
prd_title: "Player Exclusion UI Surface"
service: PlayerService
mvp_phase: 5  # Phase 5 — Compliance Surfaces
bounded_context: player

# Surface Classification (ADR-041 §5 — MANDATORY)
surface_classification:
  rendering_delivery: "Client Shell — Player 360 is already 'use client'; exclusion state is mutable during session (create/lift at any time)"
  data_aggregation: "Client-side Fetch — React Query hooks to existing REST API endpoints; single-table concern, no cross-context joins"
  rejected_patterns:
    - "RSC Prefetch + Hydration — mutable session state, rendering pattern mismatch with existing Client Shell"
    - "BFF RPC Aggregation — single-table concern, no cross-context aggregation needed"
    - "BFF Summary Endpoint — categorical enum from SQL function, not a computed summary"
  metric_provenance: "No MEAS-ID required — exclusion status is an operational state indicator (categorical enum), not a truth-bearing metric"

# Workstream Definitions
workstreams:
  WS1:
    name: Status Route Handler
    description: "GET /api/v1/players/[playerId]/exclusions/status — thin wrapper calling exclusionService.getExclusionStatus(). Completes GAP-6."
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/exclusions/status/route.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS2:
    name: React Query Exclusion Hooks
    description: "All 5 React Query hooks for exclusion data: useExclusionStatus, useExclusions, useActiveExclusions (queries) + useCreateExclusion, useLiftExclusion (mutations). Uses existing exclusion-keys.ts and exclusion-http.ts. Completes GAP-7. NOTE: Only useExclusionStatus needs the WS1 status route; the other 4 hooks target already-existing endpoints, so WS2 can run in parallel with WS1 at the code level. The dependency is retained at the phase level only because useExclusionStatus cannot be integration-tested until WS1 lands."
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - hooks/player/use-exclusions.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS3:
    name: NewSlipModal Exclusion Warning
    description: "Wire exclusionWarning display in NewSlipModal. (1) soft_alert → 10-second warning toast with AlertTriangle icon from visitResult.exclusionWarning. (2) hard_block → detect PLAYER_EXCLUDED error code from startVisit rejection, render distinct exclusion error. Completes GAP-3."
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - components/dashboard/new-slip-modal.tsx
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS4:
    name: Header Badge + Compliance Panel Exclusion Section
    description: "Read-only display surfaces. (1) Exclusion status badge in Player 360 header — severity-colored (blocked=red, alert=amber, watchlist=blue, clear=hidden). (2) Exclusion tile in compliance panel — collapsed status + active exclusion list. No Add or View History buttons in this workstream. Completes GAP-1 + GAP-2."
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2]
    outputs:
      - components/player-360/header/exclusion-status-badge.tsx
      - components/player-360/header/player-360-header-content.tsx
      - components/player-360/compliance/exclusion-tile.tsx

    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS5:
    name: Create + Lift Exclusion Dialogs
    description: "Write surfaces with role-gating. (1) Create exclusion dialog with Zod-validated form (createExclusionSchema), role-gated to pit_boss/admin via useAuth().staffRole. Adds 'Add Exclusion' trigger to exclusion-tile. (2) Lift exclusion confirmation dialog with lift_reason textarea, role-gated to admin only. Completes GAP-4 + GAP-5."
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2, WS4]
    outputs:
      - components/player-360/compliance/create-exclusion-dialog.tsx
      - components/player-360/compliance/lift-exclusion-dialog.tsx

    estimated_complexity: medium

(3) One representative mutation hook test (useCreateExclusion). (4) Component test for badge across all 4 statuses. (5) Component test for NewSlipModal soft_alert toast + hard_block rejection."
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill

      - app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/exclusions/status/__tests__/route.test.ts
      - hooks/player/__tests__/use-exclusions.test.ts
      - components/player-360/header/__tests__/exclusion-status-badge.test.tsx
      - components/dashboard/__tests__/new-slip-modal-exclusion.test.tsx
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

# Execution Phases
execution_phases:
  - name: "Phase 1 — Wire + Hooks (P0)"
    parallel: [WS1, WS2, WS3]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 2 — Display (P0)"
    parallel: [WS4]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 3 — CRUD (P1)"
    parallel: [WS5]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 4 — Tests + Final Validation (DoD)"
    parallel: [WS6]
    gates: [test-pass, lint, build]
    note: "After WS6 tests pass, lint and build gates run against the full codebase to close the DoD. All 4 defined gates (type-check, lint, test-pass, build) are enforced by the end of this phase."

# Validation Gates
gates:
  type-check:
    command: "npm run type-check 2>&1 | tail -5"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"

  lint:
    command: "npm run lint -- --quiet 2>&1 | tail -5"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, no errors"

  test-pass:
    command: "npx jest hooks/player/__tests__/use-exclusions.test.ts components/player-360/header/__tests__/exclusion-status-badge.test.tsx components/dashboard/__tests__/new-slip-modal-exclusion.test.tsx app/api/v1/players/\\[playerId\\]/exclusions/status/__tests__/route.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -20"
    success_criteria: "All tests pass, no missing files"

  build:
    command: "npm run build 2>&1 | tail -10"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"

# External Dependencies
external_dependencies:
  - prd: EXEC-050
    service: PlayerExclusionService
    required_for: "All backend (table, RLS, RPCs, service layer, API routes) — already on main"

# Risks and Mitigations
risks:
  - risk: "exclusion-http.ts getExclusionStatus() expects GET /api/v1/players/{id}/exclusions/status — route must match exactly"
    mitigation: "WS1 creates route.ts at exact path app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/exclusions/status/"
  - risk: "NewSlipModal error detection relies on PLAYER_EXCLUDED code propagation through fetchJSON"
    mitigation: "Frozen contract: domain-errors.ts:285 registers code, RPC emits RAISE EXCEPTION 'PLAYER_EXCLUDED:...', isFetchError already used in modal"
  - risk: "useAuth().staffRole may return null during initial load — dialog trigger must handle loading state"
    mitigation: "Hide create/lift buttons when staffRole is null (loading state = no buttons visible = safe default)"
  - risk: "4 of 5 HTTP fetchers in exclusion-http.ts have double-unwrap bug (DA P1-1)"
    mitigation: "WS2 pre-requisite: fix fetchers before implementing hooks"
  - risk: "is_exclusion_active() SQL marked IMMUTABLE but calls now() — planner caching risk (DA P1-3, inherited from EXEC-050)"
    mitigation: "Tracked as inherited backend debt. Not a shipping condition for this spec — requires separate backend fix ticket."
  - risk: "INV-030-7 tension — direct PostgREST DML vs Template 2b policies under pooling (DA P1-5, inherited from EXEC-050)"
    mitigation: "Tracked as inherited backend debt. Not a shipping condition for this spec — requires separate backend fix ticket."
---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-052 — Player Exclusion UI Surface

## Overview

Wire the complete player exclusion backend (already on main since `836f0ec9`) to UI surfaces within Player 360. Delivers exclusion status visibility, compliance-panel integration, CRUD dialogs for create/lift workflows, and exclusion-aware visit-start enforcement display. **No new database work** — pure frontend + 1 thin route handler. This execution spec covers PRD-052 Phases 1–3 only; Phase 4 (History / P2) remains intentionally deferred to a follow-on exec spec.

## Scope

- **In Scope**: Status route handler, React Query hooks (5), NewSlipModal exclusion warning wiring, header status badge, compliance panel exclusion section, create/lift dialogs, role-gating, tests
- **Out of Scope**: Phase 4 History sheet (P2 deferred), cross-property exclusion signals, automated expiry workflows, bulk import

## Architecture Context

- **Bounded Context**: Player (owns `player_exclusion` table per SRM)
- **Backend Inventory**: 7 service files (`exclusion-{crud,dtos,http,keys,mappers,schemas,selects}.ts`), 3 API routes (list/create, active, lift), enforcement RPC
- **Surface Pattern**: Client Shell + Client-side Fetch (ADR-041)
- **Security**: No `casino_id` from client; server-side derivation via `set_rls_context_from_staff()` (ADR-024)

## Existing Backend (Verified on Worktree)

| Layer | Files | Key Exports |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| DTOs | `services/player/exclusion-dtos.ts` | `PlayerExclusionDTO`, `CreateExclusionInput`, `LiftExclusionInput`, `ExclusionStatusDTO` |
| Schemas | `services/player/exclusion-schemas.ts` | `createExclusionSchema`, `liftExclusionSchema`, `exclusionRouteParamsSchema` |
| Keys | `services/player/exclusion-keys.ts` | `exclusionKeys.root`, `.list()`, `.active()`, `.detail()`, `.status()` |
| HTTP | `services/player/exclusion-http.ts` | `listExclusions()`, `getActiveExclusions()`, `createExclusion()`, `liftExclusion()`, `getExclusionStatus()` |
| CRUD | `services/player/exclusion-crud.ts` | All 5 operations via `createExclusionService()` |
| Routes | `app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/exclusions/` | GET list, POST create, GET active, POST lift |
| Auth | `hooks/use-auth.ts` | `useAuth()` → `{ staffRole, staffId, casinoId }` |

## Workstream Details

### WS1: Status Route Handler (GAP-6)

**Purpose**: Create the missing `GET /api/v1/players/{playerId}/exclusions/status` endpoint that `exclusion-http.ts:getExclusionStatus()` already calls.

**Deliverables**:
1. `app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/exclusions/status/route.ts` — GET handler calling `exclusionService.getExclusionStatus(playerId)`

**Pattern**: Follow existing exclusion route handlers (`exclusions/route.ts`, `exclusions/active/route.ts`) — use `withServerAction`, `createRequestContext`, `parseParams(exclusionRouteParamsSchema)`.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Returns `ExclusionStatusDTO` (`{ player_id, status }`) for all 4 statuses
- [ ] Uses `withServerAction` middleware
- [ ] No `casino_id` parameter (derived server-side)
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes

### WS2: React Query Exclusion Hooks (GAP-7)

**Purpose**: Create all React Query hooks for exclusion data fetching and mutation.

**Deliverables**:
1. `hooks/player/use-exclusions.ts` containing:
   - `useExclusionStatus(playerId)` — query using `exclusionKeys.status()` + `getExclusionStatus()`
   - `useExclusions(playerId)` — query using `exclusionKeys.list()` + `listExclusions()`
   - `useActiveExclusions(playerId)` — query using `exclusionKeys.active()` + `getActiveExclusions()`
   - `useCreateExclusion()` — mutation calling `createExclusion()`, invalidates `list`, `active`, `status` keys
   - `useLiftExclusion()` — mutation calling `liftExclusion()`, invalidates `list`, `active`, `status` keys

**Pattern**: Follow existing hook patterns (e.g., `hooks/player/use-player.ts`). Use `useQuery` for reads, `useMutation` + `useQueryClient` for writes with targeted cache invalidation.

**Pre-requisite (DA P1-1)**: Fix 4 broken HTTP fetchers in `exclusion-http.ts` before implementing hooks. `listExclusions`, `getActiveExclusions`, `createExclusion`, `liftExclusion` double-unwrap the `fetchJSON` envelope — change from `fetchJSON<{ data: T }>(...); return response.data` to `return fetchJSON<T>(...)` (matching the correct `getExclusionStatus` pattern).

**Mutation Variable Types**:
- `useCreateExclusion().mutate({ playerId: string, input: Omit<CreateExclusionInput, 'player_id'> })`
- `useLiftExclusion().mutate({ playerId: string, exclusionId: string, input: LiftExclusionInput })`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All 5 hooks export correctly
- [ ] HTTP fetcher double-unwrap bug fixed (4 fetchers return data, not `undefined`)
- [ ] Mutations invalidate `exclusionKeys.list(playerId)`, `exclusionKeys.active(playerId)`, `exclusionKeys.status(playerId)` for the affected `playerId`
- [ ] All query hooks use `staleTime: 30_000` (matching codebase convention) and `enabled: !!playerId` guard
- [ ] Mutation variable types are explicitly typed per signatures above
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes

### WS3: NewSlipModal Exclusion Warning (GAP-3)

**Purpose**: Wire the already-available `exclusionWarning` field from `startVisit()` response to user-visible feedback, and detect `hard_block` rejections.

**Deliverables**:
1. Modified `components/dashboard/new-slip-modal.tsx`:
   - After `visitResult.resumed` toast block (~line 218), add: if `visitResult.exclusionWarning` → `toast.warning('Exclusion Alert', { description: visitResult.exclusionWarning, icon: <AlertTriangle />, duration: 10_000 })`
   - In `handleSubmit` catch block, add: if `isFetchError(err) && err.code === 'PLAYER_EXCLUDED'` → `setError('This player has an active exclusion and cannot be seated')` (distinct from generic error)

**Frozen Contracts**:
- `StartVisitResultDTO.exclusionWarning: string | null` (services/visit/dtos.ts:152)
- `PLAYER_EXCLUDED` error code (lib/errors/domain-errors.ts:285)
- `isFetchError` utility (already imported in modal)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `soft_alert` visit shows amber warning toast with 10-second duration
- [ ] `hard_block` rejection shows distinct exclusion error message (not generic error)
- [ ] Existing visit flow (no exclusion) unchanged
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes

### WS4: Header Badge + Compliance Panel Exclusion Section (GAP-1, GAP-2)

**Purpose**: Add read-only exclusion status surfaces to Player 360.

**Deliverables**:
1. `components/player-360/header/exclusion-status-badge.tsx` — severity-colored badge:
   - `blocked` → red (`bg-red-500/10 text-red-400 border-red-500/30`, `ShieldX` or `Ban` icon)
   - `alert` → amber (`bg-amber-500/10 text-amber-400 border-amber-500/30`, `AlertTriangle` icon)
   - `watchlist` → blue (`bg-blue-500/10 text-blue-400 border-blue-500/30`, `Eye` icon)
   - `clear` → hidden (no badge rendered)
2. Modified `components/player-360/header/player-360-header-content.tsx` — add `ExclusionStatusBadge` next to enrollment badge (line ~283)
3. `components/player-360/compliance/exclusion-tile.tsx` — collapsed status tile + active exclusion list (read-only; no Add or View History buttons)
4. Modified `components/player-360/compliance/panel.tsx` — add `ExclusionTile` above CTR progress

**Pattern**: Follow `CtrProgressTile` color conventions. Badge follows existing enrollment badge pattern. Use `useExclusionStatus` and `useActiveExclusions` from WS2.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Badge renders correct color for all 4 statuses
- [ ] Badge hidden when status is `clear`
- [ ] WCAG 2.1 AA contrast for badge colors against dark background
- [ ] Exclusion tile shows enforcement level and active count
- [ ] Active exclusions listed with type, enforcement, dates, reason preview
- [ ] No Add or View History buttons
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes

### WS5: Create + Lift Exclusion Dialogs (GAP-4, GAP-5)

**Purpose**: CRUD write surfaces with role-gating.

**Deliverables**:
1. `components/player-360/compliance/create-exclusion-dialog.tsx`:
   - Form fields from `createExclusionSchema`: exclusion_type (select), enforcement (select), reason (textarea), effective_from (date), effective_until (date, optional), review_date (date, optional), external_ref (input, optional), jurisdiction (input, optional)
   - Validates via `createExclusionSchema`
   - Uses `useCreateExclusion()` mutation from WS2
   - React Hook Form + Zod resolver (consistent with `PlayerEditModal` pattern)
2. `components/player-360/compliance/lift-exclusion-dialog.tsx`:
   - Confirmation dialog showing exclusion details
   - `lift_reason` textarea (1–1000 chars, validated via `liftExclusionSchema`)
   - Uses `useLiftExclusion()` mutation from WS2
3. Modified `components/player-360/compliance/exclusion-tile.tsx`:
   - "Add Exclusion" button, visible only for `pit_boss`/`admin` roles (`staffRole === 'pit_boss' || staffRole === 'admin'`) — uses allowlist matching RLS INSERT policy, NOT denylist
   - "Lift" button per active exclusion row, visible only for `admin` role (`staffRole === 'admin'`)

**Role-Gating Pattern**: Use `useAuth().staffRole` to conditionally render trigger buttons. Buttons are hidden (not disabled) for unauthorized roles.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Create dialog validates input against `createExclusionSchema`
- [ ] Create button visible only for `pit_boss`/`admin` (allowlist, not denylist — matches RLS INSERT policy)
- [ ] Lift dialog requires `lift_reason` (1–1000 chars)
- [ ] Lift button hidden for non-`admin` roles
- [ ] Mutations fire correctly and UI refreshes via cache invalidation
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes

### WS6: Tests

**Purpose**: Satisfy DoD testing requirements.

**Deliverables** (matches PRD DoD Section 8 — Testing):
1. `app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/exclusions/status/__tests__/route.test.ts` — route handler integration test (`/** @jest-environment node */` docblock required)
2. `hooks/player/__tests__/use-exclusions.test.ts` — one representative query hook test (`useExclusionStatus`) + one representative mutation hook test (`useCreateExclusion`)
3. `components/player-360/header/__tests__/exclusion-status-badge.test.tsx` — badge rendering across all 4 statuses
4. `components/dashboard/__tests__/new-slip-modal-exclusion.test.tsx` — soft_alert toast + hard_block error

**Pattern**: Follow existing test patterns:
- Route handler tests: see `app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/__tests__/route.test.ts` (note `@jest-environment node`)
- Component tests: see `components/player-360/__tests__/` directory
- Hook tests: mock `fetchJSON`, verify cache key usage

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Route handler test covers all 4 statuses with `@jest-environment node`
- [ ] Hook test covers `useExclusionStatus` (query) and `useCreateExclusion` (mutation)
- [ ] Badge test covers all 4 statuses including hidden for `clear`
- [ ] Modal test covers soft_alert toast and hard_block error display
- [ ] No regressions in existing tests

## Frozen Rules

1. **Active visit + post-hoc exclusion (ADR-042 D2)**: No modal enforcement after visit creation. Compliance panel may show passive indicator.
2. **PLAYER_EXCLUDED error code**: Registered in `domain-errors.ts:285`, emitted by RPC. UI branches on code, not message text.
3. **Immutability (ADR-042 D3)**: Exclusions cannot be modified — only lifted.

## DA Team Review (Attempt 1 — WARN, Ship w/ gates)

All 5 reviewers returned **Ship w/ gates** (0 P0, 5 P1, 7 P2). Spec revised to incorporate all actionable findings:

| Reviewer | Verdict | Key Findings Applied |
|----------|---------|---------------------|
| R1 Security | Ship w/ gates | INV-030-7 flagged as inherited risk; SEC-001 doc gap noted |
| R2 Architecture | Ship w/ gates | Role-gate changed from denylist to allowlist |
| R3 Implementation | Ship w/ gates | HTTP fetcher bug fix added as WS2 pre-req; mutation types specified |
| R4 Test Quality | Ship w/ gates | `--passWithNoTests` removed; `@jest-environment node` mandated; test scope aligned to PRD DoD |
| R5 Performance | Ship w/ gates | `staleTime: 30_000` added; `is_exclusion_active` IMMUTABLE→STABLE tracked as debt |

**Inherited backend debt (not EXEC-052 scope — separate fix tickets)**:
- `is_exclusion_active()` marked IMMUTABLE but calls `now()` — needs `ALTER FUNCTION ... STABLE` migration (R5 P1)
- INV-030-7 tension: direct PostgREST DML vs Template 2b session-var-only policies (R1 P1)
- `lifted_by` column may need DB DEFAULT or CRUD fix (R2 P2)
- `player_exclusion` missing from SEC-001/SEC-002 docs (R1 P2)
- `player_exclusion` missing from ADR-030 Category A/B registry (R1 P2)

## Definition of Done

- [ ] All 6 workstreams complete
- [ ] All gates pass (type-check, lint, test, build)
- [ ] No regressions in existing tests
- [ ] Security: no `casino_id` from client, role-gating correct
- [ ] Surface classification declared (ADR-041 §5 compliant)
